contraindicationNote: Contraindicated in active liver disease or unexplained persistent elevations in serum transaminases

childPughCategories.recommendation: Use with caution or avoid; substantial hepatic impairment increases simvastatin exposure
Avoid; contraindicated in severe hepatic impairment
Monitor INR more frequently; liver disease may affect vitamin K clotting factor synthesis
Use with caution; significant hepatic disease may unpredictably affect warfarin anticoagulant effect; frequent INR monitoring required
Use with extreme caution; severe hepatic impairment greatly increases bleeding risk; consider alternative anticoagulation
No significant dose adjustment typically required
Consider dose reduction; metoprolol is extensively hepatically metabolized (CYP2D6)
Significant dose reduction required; hepatic impairment markedly increases metoprolol exposure
Max 20mg/day; hepatic impairment reduces omeprazole metabolism
Max 20mg/day; use lowest effective dose due to significantly reduced hepatic clearance
Start at lower dose (2.5-5mg); titrate slowly
Start at 2.5mg; hepatic impairment significantly increases amlodipine half-life
Start at 2.5mg; monitor closely; exposure significantly increased with severe hepatic impairment
Use with caution; risk of hepatic encephalopathy with rapid fluid/electrolyte changes
Initiate slowly; high risk of hepatic encephalopathy; start low, titrate carefully
